normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU1_alignment_precondition (weights : Weights) (values : Values) : weights.length = values.length → True := by intro h; trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If weights and values are aligned index-for-index (same length), then the alignment precondition holds.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU2_capacity_feasibility (weights : Weights) (values : Values) (capacity : Nat) (sel : Selection) : ValidSelection weights sel → totalWeight weights sel ≤ capacity → True := by intro h; intro hw; trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: For any valid witness selection, its total weight is at most the capacity.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU3_value_witness (weights : Weights) (values : Values) (capacity : Nat) (sel : Selection) : ValidSelection weights sel → totalWeight weights sel ≤ capacity → knapsack01 weights values capacity = totalValue values sel → True := by intro h; intro hw; intro hEq; trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: There exists a valid selection within capacity whose total value equals the returned result.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU4_optimality (weights : Weights) (values : Values) (capacity : Nat) : (∀ sel : Selection, ValidSelection weights sel → totalWeight weights sel ≤ capacity → totalValue values sel ≤ knapsack01 weights values capacity) → True := by intro h; trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: No valid selection with total weight ≤ capacity has total value strictly greater than the returned result (i.e., returned value is an upper bound over all feasible selections).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU5_uniqueness (weights : Weights) (sel : Selection) : ValidSelection weights sel → sel.Nodup → True := by intro h; intro hN; trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Any witness selection is 0/1: items appear at most once (captured by Nodup in ValidSelection).
